titlestmt.titleproper: Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century."23-2-2720

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 01:54:03 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century."

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology

did.physdesc: Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University.
did.abstract: Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. Arrangements/Publicity Box 1 Folders 62-76Box 2 Folders 1-17

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century," #23-2-2720. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Panelists/Attendees
Allen, Ruth - commentary and correspondence
Apple, Rina - University of Wisconsin - "Every Girl a Mother Home Economics and the Ideology of Motherhood, 1890-1950"
Babbitt, Kathleen - SUNY Binghamton - "Alphabet Soup and Depression-Era Subsistence: NYS Extension and the New Deal Agencies of the 1930s"
Bates, Mercedes correspondence
Belesco, Warren - University of Maryland, Baltimore County - "Home Economists vs. Alternative Foodways"
Brumberg, J.J. - vita and correspondence
Bryant, Keith - Cornell University - Comments on Horowitz and Robinson Papers
Cravens, Hamilton - Iowa State University - correspondence and vita
Colman, Gould - Cornell University Archivist
Conable, Charlotte W. - resume and correspondence
Cowan, Ruth Schwartz - SUNY, Stony Brook (facilitator/respondent)
Davis, Audrey - Curator, The National Museum of American History
Dean Firebaugh correspondence
DeVault, Eileen - ILR, Cornell University
Dudden, Faye E. - Union College (facilitator/respondent)
East, Marjorie - panelist - correspondence and resume
Furness, Betty
Geistfeld, Loren - Ohio State University - Vita
Gentzler, Yvonne S. - resume and correspondence
Goldsmith, Elizabeth - Invited Scholar - Florida State University
Goldstein, Carolyn - University of Delaware - "Part of the Package? : Home Economists in Industry in the Interwar Period"
Grant, Julia - Boston - "Modernizing Motherhood: Modernizing Motherhood and the Parent Education Movement, 1915-1940"
Haber, Barbara - Schlesinger Library, Radcliffe College - "'You and Chocolate Cake': Betty Crocker's Message to Females in the 1950s" and recipes
Harris, Carmen V. - Clemson University - "Grace Under Pressure: The Black Home Economics Agent in South Carolina, 1919-1967"
Hopkins, Bernice
Horowitz, Daniel - Smith College - "The Cultural Construction of Spending: American Household Budget Studies in the Early 20th Century"
James, Rae Jean - biography, correspondence, resume
Kline, Ronald - Cornell University - "Agents of Modernity: Home Economics and Rural Electrification in the United States, 1925-1950"
Knowles, Jane B. - University of Wisconsin - "Domestic Science and Rural Women in the Midwest, 1900-1920 Two Examples from the Land Grant University Community"
Khodtedt, Sally Gregory - University of Minnesota (facilitator/respondent)
Lane, Ann J. - University of Virginia (facilitator/respondent)
Levenstein, Harvey - McMaster University - "Home Economists and Ethnic Food"
Low, Florence (commentator) - biography, correspondence, newspaper article
Mapes, Martha - correspondence and vita
McCray, Jacqueline - University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff - Correspondence
Meyer, Sibylle & Schulze, Eva - "Technology and Family: A Contribution to the Impact of Gender on Technological Development"
Michel, Sonya - "A New Deal for Home Economics"
Nerad, Maresi - University of California, Berkeley - "Gender and Status at the University of California at Berkeley: The Case of Home Economics 1918-1954"
Newkirk, Gwendolyn - University of Nebraska, Lincoln
Nyhart, Lynn - University of Wisconsin, Madison - "Home Economics in the American Hospital, 1900-1930"
Past, Lois (Invited Scholar)
Reed, Hazel - biography, resume, correspondence
Ricciuti, Henry - commentator on Rima Apple's and Julia Grant's papers
Robinson, Lisa - Michigan State University - "Safeguarded by your Refrigerator: Mary Engle Pennington and Industrial Home Economics"
Ross, Alice - Hearth Studios - vitae and brochure
Rossiter, Margaret - "Men in Home Economics - A Contradiction in Terms?"
Sicherman, Barbara - Trinity College (facilitator/respondent)
Sklar, Kitty - SUNY Binghamton
Smith, Ed - "Anna Bostford Comstock: Artist, Author, and Teacher"
Stage, Sarah - UC Riverside - "'Home is not in the Kitchen' A New Look at Ellen Richards and the Home Economics Movement"
St. Marie, Satening - JC Penny Forum
Thomas, Genevieve W.
Tomes, Nancy Jane - vita
Vincent, Alison - "Theoretic Studies of 'Philanthropic' Interests of Women Educators"
Vincenti, Virginia (panelist/facilitator)
Washington, Margaret - "Home Economics, Industrial Education and the Penn School Challenge, 1863-1930"
Weber, Rose Marie - SUNY Albany - vita and correspondence
Weigly, Emma S.
White, Suzanne - FDA - "'One Third Essence of Nothing': Home Economics as Consumer Advocates in the Cornell Bread Controversy, 1948-1950"
Williams, E. Zabe - University of Wisconsin, Madison - "'Education for Living': The Definitions and Designs of the Lake Placid Conference on Home Economics, 1899-1908"
Ziegler, Jerome M. - correspondence
Series II. Arrangements/Publicity
Mailing lists
Home economists - possible conference participants
Attendance records and final list of participants
Panel of Home Economists, speakers, commentators
Returned contracts
Request forms and memos regarding room reservations
Budget/Receipts
Caterers - correspondence, menus
Exhibit at Olin
Home Economics in film - An evening of historical films by and about the profession - from the National Archives and others
Information on the Statler and local motels
Official file
Parking
Funding - National Endowment for the Humanities and Cornell University
Names and Addresses of People requesting copies of papers/tapes
New York Council for the Humanities - correspondence, funding, travel, Joan's final report
Publicity - news releases, newspaper articles, mailings
Correspondence regarding the Wolcott Letters
Correspondence regarding the 65th anniversary of the College of Human Ecology
Correspondence regarding possible material to be presented at conference
Dean's office correspondence
Correspondence with politicians
General correspondence to Brumberg and Bergstrom
Responses to invitations to the conference
Responses from participants about the Conference
Applications for interpretive research (Endowment for the Humanities) and grants (National Science Foundation)
Conference coordinator
External advisory group for conference
Early home economics pamphlets
"Pioneers in Home Economics" by Flora Rose, Home Economics Bibliography, and "The Beginnings of Child Development and Family Life Education in the 20th Century" Lawrence Frank,
Folder of information for the conference
Blueprints for agenda for conference printed at Typographics
Prints and copies of old photographs
Series III. Cassette Tapes
1st session of conference
Alumni Auditorium 1:20pm
Alumni Auditorium 3:00pm
Alumni Auditorium 4:45pm

Intro by Eileen DeVault, Lynn Nyhart: "Home Economists in the Hospital, 1900-1930," Carolyn Goldstein: "Part of the Package: Home Economists in Industry During the Interwar Period," Lois Post: commentary - what is was like to be Betty Crocker
Intro by SG Khohlstedt, Sarah Stage on Ellen Swallow Richards and the Lake Placid conferences, Nancy Tomes "Spreading the Germ Theory"

Intro by Margaret Washington, Rose Marie Weber: "Starved for Something to Read: Literacy in the Cornell Reading Course for Farmers' Wives," Cramen Harris: "Grace Under Pressure: The African-American Home Economics Agent in South Carolina, 1919-1965," comment by Florence Low on papers and on black home economics agents in Texas in the 1930s, during WWII and during integration

Intro by Ann Lane, Rima Apple: "Every Girl a Mother: Home Economics and the Ideology of Motherhood, 1890-1950," Julia Grant: "Modernizing Mothers: Home Economics and the Parent Eduction Movement," Comments: Henry Ricciuti and Lane
Hersey
